steps.title: Apply the preservation rule
steps.content: The passage says wood exposed to air and water rots quickly unless sediment protects it from oxygen. Since much of the sixteenth-century ship's wooden hull survived in the flooded quarry, it was likely shielded by sediment.

steps.title: Reject unsupported inferences
steps.content: Choice B speculates about other ships in the quarry. Choice C shifts to theories about the type of wood, which the passage never discusses. Choice D suggests an earlier construction date, but preservation by sediment has no logical connection to changing the ship's age. The answer is A.
